seoTitle: Orangeville vs Orient Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Orangeville and Orient Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Orangeville vs Orient Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Orangeville and Orient Point.
heading: Orangeville vs Orient Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Orangeville, New South Wales and Orient Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Orient Point has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Orangeville (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Orangeville or Orient Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Orangeville has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Orient Point scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Orangeville scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Orient Point scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Orangeville or Orient Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Orient Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
